mnemonics: BEACONS: Bias, Explainability, Accountability, Control, Opacity, Norms, Security - the seven core risk pillars to watch for in AI ethics and policy.
keyPoints: AI systems can perpetuate and amplify societal biases, leading to unfair outcomes.
A lack of transparency and explainability ('black box' problem) makes it difficult to audit AI decisions or assign responsibility.
The autonomous nature of AI can lead to a 'responsibility gap' where no single actor is held accountable for harm.
commonMistakes: Assuming AI is inherently neutral or objective, when it often reflects the biases in its training data.
Focusing solely on technical performance metrics while ignoring broader ethical and societal impacts.
Treating AI governance as a one-time compliance exercise rather than an ongoing process of risk management.
